title: Ma'w'shiye
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}}

{{Quote|Though I am thankful he has not chosen to take sides with the Empire, he must be brought to justice: if not for the safety of all those he endangers, then at least so that I may strip from him the lieutenant's insignia, Trand Medallion and Platinum Valor Cross I personally pinned upon his lapel.|General Airen Cracken, on Ma'w'shiye|<ref name="aj4" />}}
'''Ma'w'shiye''' was an expert [[Scout/Legends|scout]], a graduate of the [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]] and later a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic's]] [[Eclipse Team]] and [[Task Force on Alliance Security|security task force]].

A [[M'shento'su'Nikto]] native to [[Kintan/Legends|Kintan]], he had enrolled in [[Barosa Warren]]'s Galactic Outdoor Survival School during the early reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], learning to survive in hostile environments and graduating as part of the very successful [[Twilight Class]] alongside the likes of [[Sisquoc]], [[Akul Witig]], [[Adazian Liebke]] and [[Derembus Sitnalta]] in [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]]. [[0 BBY/Legends|Several years later]], Ma'w'shiye joined the Rebel Alliance with his peers, serving with distinction in the elite [[Twilight Squadron (Rebel)|Twilight Squadron]] for six years during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was decorated many times by his commanding [[Officer/Legends|officers]], and won the respect of his peers and of the recruits he trained.

Suddenly and without explanation, Ma'w'shiye deserted his [[Mission to Sanjin|covert operation]] team in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], and then initiated a series of [[Assassin/Legends|assassinations]] and assassination attempts against his previous partners in the Twilight Squadron during the first years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] posted a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Ma'w'shiye's head, under charges of [[Treason/Legends|treason]] and others, and organized a SpecForces team to track him down.

==Biography==
===Origins===
{{Quote|We didn't just break the Summit Class record; we absolutely destroyed it!|Ma'w'shiye, on the graduation of the Twilight Class|<ref name="air" />}}
A [[M'shento'su'Nikto]]<ref name="gg12" /> born on [[Kintan/Legends|Kintan]],<ref name="aj4" /> Ma'w'shiye trained to became a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] at the prestigious [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]],<ref name="air" /> where he underwent intense survival training, learning to survive on any kind of world.<ref name="reb">[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']]; [[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref> Ma'w'shiye was a member of the prestigious [[Twilight Class]], alongside other prodiges such as [[Adazian Liebke]], [[Norrion Glidamir]], [[Xenon Nnaksta]], [[Sisquoc]], [[Derembus Sitnalta]] and [[Akul Witig]]. All the members of the Twilight Class became expert scouts under the supervision of GOSS headmaster [[Barosa Warren]].<ref name="air" />

In [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]], the Twilight Class graduated with honors, breaking the previous GOSS record established by the [[Summit Class]] years before. Due to this, Summit veteran [[Lunkar An]] felt his ego threatened and became an enemy of the Twilight Class veterans. Although An considered himself Sisquoc's personal nemesis, he felt a particular hatred towards Ma'w'shiye; their mutual dislike caused An and Ma'w'shiye to fight each other many times.<ref name="air" />

===The Alliance===
[[File:Mawshiye-AIR.png|thumb|left|200px|Ma'w'shiye as a member of the [[Task Force on Alliance Security]]]]
{{Quote|Barosa's dominance as a survivalist goes without question, but those of you who stand a chance of encountering him in combat of any short should also keep in mind his weaponry.|Extract from Ma'w'shiye's addendum to the Alliance threat report on Barosa Warren|<ref name="air" />}}
Ma'w'shiye joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] around [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="air" /> He became a [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]],<ref name="gg12" /><ref name="air" /> with the insignia being pinned to his chest by Rebel [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]].<ref name="aj4" /> Ma'w'shiye was then assigned to the [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]] elite [[Eclipse Team]]'s [[Twilight Squadron]] as a marksman,<ref name="gg12" /> along with other Twilight Class veterans including Liebke, Sisquoc, Sitnalta and Witig. Maw'shiye also performed field work, becoming a veteran in different dangerous missions, and he also worked as a weapon coordinator. Alongside Derembus Sitnalta, who had became a [[Commander/Legends|Commander]], Ma'w'shiye trained recruits in the use of high-powered blasters.<ref name="air" /> During all his years in the Alliance, Maw'shiye came to train literally thousands of them not only in the use of light artillery, but also of [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]].<ref name="aj4" />

Ma'w'shiye fought in the [[Attack on the Pirik system|Attacks on Corint City]], defending the [[Pirik system/Legends|Pirik system]] from an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] invasion.<ref name="air" /> Forced to escape from the Empire through the glass window of [[Strak Tower]]'s 37th floor, Ma'w'shiye almost lost his right leg during this operation. The leg was saved but, from that point on, Ma'w'shiye had a visible limp.<ref name="aj4" /> Due to his performance in Pirik, he became a living legend in the Alliance gunnery ranks.<ref name="air" />

He also took part in the [[Karideph operations]], during which his friend Witig presented him a [[Ma'w'shiye's heavy overcoat|heavy overcoat]] that he would don on when on cold environments from that point on. Ma'w'shiye was also a member of the Alliance team that defended the two last outposts of the [[Dnalvec Militia]] on [[Sriluur/Legends|Sriluur]] against the Galactic Empire.<ref name="aj4" />

As a member of the [[Task Force on Alliance Security]], Ma'w'shiye wrote reports on threats to the Rebel Alliance. During this time, he worked with his previous mates Liebke and Witig, as well as with other Rebel officers including Lieutenant [[Alton Lochner]], Lieutenant Command [[Sasnak Toxis]] and agents [[Tirranna]], and [[Tynan Ryln Mei]]. Ma'w'shiye collaborated in the report on Barosa Warren, praising his survivalist skill and weaponry. Ma'w'shiye had witnessed his colleague [[Maashan]]'s [[XP-38 sport landspeeder]] failing to provide a suitable cover for Warren's target and, as such, the Nikto reported that people opposing Warren should avoid using [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeders]] as cover.<ref name="air" />

The Alliance highly valued Ma'w'shiye's performance as a weapon coordinator, special forces operative and [[Pathfinder-class scout ship|pathfinder]] in dangerous tasks, and he was known to have served courageously side to side with Liebke and Witig. Between 0 BBY and [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Ma'w'shiye's meritorious actions led to him being commended and decored several times. Cracken awarded him with the [[Trand Medallion]] and the [[Platinum Valor Cross]].<ref name="aj4" />

===The fugitive===
{{Quote|Nothing disturbs me greater than to see one of the Alliance's best go against his own comrades.|General Airen Cracken, on Ma'w'shiye|<ref name="aj4" />}}
In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], just before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Ma'w'shiye was part of a [[Mission to Sanjin|covert operation]] team on [[Sanjin/Legends|Sanjin]], near the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. For reasons unknown to his partners and officers, Ma'w'shiye stole the team's escape [[Starship/Legends|starship]], deserting his squadron and leading to the failure of the operation. Ma'w'shiye's betrayal was expensive to the Alliance, as it cost more lives and gear than any other operation of its size.<ref name="aj4" />

From that point on, Ma'w'shiye turned against his previous partners and allies. He unsuccessfully tried to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] his previous associates Liebke, Lochner, Nnaksta, Sitnalta, Tirranna, Toxis, Tynan and Witig, who had become officers of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]—the galactic government created by members of the Alliance. He managed to kill other New Republic agents including [[Candra]], [[Klepish]] and [[Saloor]], as well as at least seventy-two non-affiliated civilians. New Republic analysts were surprised at this low figure, as Ma'w'shiye was such a skilled [[Sniper/Legends|sniper]] that he could have killed many more if he wished. [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] confirmed that Ma'w'shiye had not joined the Galactic Empire.<ref name="aj4" />

Nonetheless, the New Republic branded Ma'w'shiye as a threat to its members, offering a 70,000-[[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] for him on the charges of assassination, assault on New Republic personnel with intention to kill, destruction and theft of New Republic property and treason. Airen Cracken, by then a [[General/Legends|General]] and head of New Republic Intelligence, was personally disgusted at Ma'w'shiye's attempts against the lives of his previous partners, and wanted the Nikto captured so he could take back the medals he himself had granted. A New Republic SpecForces team was formed to track and capture Ma'w'shiye, and the survivors of Ma'w'shiye's assassination attempts were assigned to lead it.<ref name="aj4" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|If I ever catch him, he'll be in no condition to face the Survival Zones when I'm through with him.|Ma'w'shiye, on the threat posed by Lunkar An|<ref name="air" />}}
Ma'w'shiye was a muscled M'shento'su'Nikto. Like others of his [[Species/Legends|species]], he had dark, black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] covered with a natural cheratin-like substance that prevented blurry sight under water or during sandstorms. Ma'w'shiye's skin was rust-colored, but during his career he got a lot of white-purple scars. Unlike most M'shento'su'Niktos, Ma'w'shiye lacked the usual minute horns surrounding his face, because he had lost them in a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] accident in the rock canyons on [[Miglar 2]] at some point of his career.<ref name="aj4" />

Ma'w'shiye was an excellent scout, particularly expert as a tracker and pathfinder. He was also a peerless sniper with several kinds of blaster weapons, either personal or vehicle. Should the need arise, Ma'w'shiye could resort to melee fighting, another area of expertise<ref name="aj4" />—to the point that he was not afraid at all of fighting the brawler Lunkar An.<ref name="air" /> An accomplished speeder bike [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], Ma'w'shiye specialized in [[Aratech Repulsor Company/Legends|Aratech Repulsor Company]] [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|74-Z speeder bikes]]. Lastly, he was an expert in demolitions and repairing [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], particularly the [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech]] [[EE-3 carbine rifle/Legends|EE-3 carbine rifle]] and [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] [[XC24]] models.<ref name="aj4" />

==Equipment==
Ma'w'shiye partially changed his equipment from the moment he deserted the Alliance. When he was a rebel lieutenant, he used a loose-fitting jerking and combat trousers that had became his personal trademark. He also favored service boots, an Alliance-issue utility belt, munition straps and a special issue ammo that he could obtain as an officer of the Alliance. In cold environments, he used an overcoat that Akul Witig had gifted him with.<ref name="aj4" />

During the days of the New Republic, he stopped using his previously-characteristic jerking and trousers. He had also replaced his favorite ammunition with another, more easy-to-obtain one. His weapons of choice included a [[Force pike/Legends|force pike]], a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]] and a [[Heavy blaster rifle/Legends|heavy blaster rifle]] to which he had added a [[Tru-Vu 4 Scope]].<ref name="aj4" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Mawshiye-SWR.png|thumb|Ma'w'shiye as a Kadas'sa'Nikto]]
First appearing in the article [[Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 4|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' #4]], by [[Craig Robert Carey|C. Robert Carey]], Ma'w'shiye was depicted as a M'shento'su'Nikto in an illustration by [[Robert Duchlinski]].<ref name="aj4" /> Ma'w'shiye was then mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', co-written by Carey, and including a picture by [[Mike Vilardi]] that followed Duchlinski's previous characterization.<ref name="air" /> Then, in ''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]'', Ma'w'shiye is textually identified as a M'shento'su'Nikto.<ref name="gg12" />

The article includes information extrapolated from his [[roleplaying game]] template from ''Wanted by Cracken.'' The stats in the non-canon video game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], that cannot be so easily transplanted to a textual article, show Ma'w'shiye as having excellent combat and espionage talents, but being a sub-par diplomat and leader. In the game, Ma'w'shiye could not become an officer or research new technologies, and he had an average possibility of being [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]].<ref name="reb" />

According to designer [[Craig Robert Carey]],  [[Ma'w'shiye]]'s unnamed [[Heavy blaster rifle/Legends|heavy blaster rifle]] was an XC24.<ref name="DeckPlanAlliance">{{WebCite|url=http://deckplans.00sf.com/Research/Q_and_A.html|text=SoroSuub XC24 Heavy Blaster Rifle|work=Star Wars Deckplans Alliance|quote=Oh man, that was ages ago. But yes, the XC24 was intended to be the heavy blaster rifle (the one shown in the Wanted entry)|archivedate=20250210001305}}</ref>

===Inconsistencies===
{{Quote|The "Southern Nikto," or ''M'shento'su'Nikto,'' have white, yellow or orange skin. They lack horns entirely, but have multitude of breather tubes.|Galaxy Guide 12, pages 60-61|<ref name="gg12" />}}
{{Quote|The minute horns about his forehead and face typical of his species are nearly completely absent, a result of a speeder bike accident in the rock canyons of Miglar 2.|Star Wars Adventure Journal #4, page 230|<ref name="aj4" />}}
''Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens - Enemies and Allies'' also specifies that M'shento'su'Niktos lacked natural horns,<ref name="gg12" /> but ''Wanted by Cracken'' specifically said that Ma'w'shiye had had natural horns before his stay on Miglar 2.<ref name="aj4" />

In the ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' video game, his portrait corresponds to a [[Kadas'sa'Nikto/Legends|Kadas'sa'Nikto]]. This game, graphically inaccurate, had also misused a portrait of [[Bob Hudsol/Legends|Bob Hudsol]] to illustrate the character of [[Vanden Willard/Legends|Vanden Willard]].<ref name="reb" />

==Appearances==
